motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Clear and cold, 22°F at first light, climbing no higher than 27°F by afternoon. The sky is a hard, particular blue that belongs only to the first real cold of the season. The lake ice has thickened overnight to one and three-quarter inches.

undercurrent.note: Still ordinary in every visible way. The shelving unit has been ordered. The twenty-three items in the back room — 'some I have not yet had occasion to open' — are now in the record, and things that get into the record have a way of becoming the next thing. Calloway has noted the alcove catches morning light in winter and makes the dust motes visible; she swept, she says. Level nudges to 10 — the thread is slightly more there, the way a door is slightly more open than it was yesterday, though to look at it you would call it closed. Stage holds at 0. The books that will appear have not appeared yet. They are patient.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: The library's east alcove has been empty since 2009, when the last cataloguer left in the middle of a Wednesday with no explanation. Calloway has begun shelving it. The books that appear on the new shelves overnight are not books the library owns, and they are not books that have ever been published, but they are about Greywater Falls — and they are set in years that have not happened yet.
